water_temp_read.read: Water is warming; trout may favor more oxygenated water.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Current trout fishing quality is fair to good with warming water conditions and recent stocking supporting multiple fly options. Expect trout to favor oxygenated runs and pocket water especially during early morning.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning is best due to calm winds, low sky cover, and cool temperatures that promote trout activity in riffles and oxygenated runs, matching the angler's recommended early fishing tactics.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 62.8°F with a flow of 899 cfs and gage height at 2.03 ft. Turbidity is low at 5.9 NTU, providing clear water conditions.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current sky cover averages 20%, pressure is steady at 30.05 inHg with no significant trend. Winds are from the west at 5.8 mph with gusts up to 17.3 mph. Rain probability is low with minimal expected precipitation.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely to stay near riffles, pocket water, and runs with good oxygenation, feeding actively but cautiously under light to moderate sky cover. Use clean drifts with smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell nymphs. Early mornings and late windows offer the best chances with active and balanced nymph presentations.
